objective: Implement a fully functional AI-powered learning chatbot. The agent MUST: (1) search GitHub for a well-designed open-source React chat widget and adapt its code, (2) implement a Django REST API that calls the Anthropic Claude API, and (3) wire everything together in the existing project. All files and code paths are provided explicitly below.

project_context.frontend_framework: React 18 (create-react-app), axios (via api.js), lucide-react icons, CSS custom properties for theming
project_context.auth_model: core.User (custom user model), JWT tokens stored in localStorage under key 'tokens' — format: { access, refresh }

models.ChatSession: id(UUID PK), user(FK core.User), course(FK courses.Course nullable), created_at, updated_at — db_table='chat_sessions'
models.ChatMessage: id(UUID PK), session(FK ChatSession), role(USER|BOT), content(TextField), source_lecture(optional str), confidence(optional float), is_helpful(optional bool), created_at — db_table='chat_messages', ordering=['created_at']

project_context.existing_main_urls: backend/backend/urls.py currently has: path('admin/', ...), path('api/', include('core.urls')), path('api/courses/', include('courses.urls'))
project_context.existing_api_js_exports: authAPI, courseAPI — the chatbotAPI export must be APPENDED to this file

steps.instruction:
At the very top of the file, AFTER 'import os' and AFTER 'from pathlib import Path', insert these two lines:

from dotenv import load_dotenv
load_dotenv()

Then at the very bottom of the file add:

# Anthropic AI API
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY = os.environ.get('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY', '')

tasks.title: Backend: Create chatbot/urls.py and register it in main urls.py
tasks.description: The chatbot app has no urls.py. Create it, then register it in the main urlconf under /api/chatbot/.

steps.exact_content:
from django.urls import path
from .views import ChatbotMessageView, ChatbotHistoryView

urlpatterns = [
    path('message/', ChatbotMessageView.as_view(), name='chatbot-message'),
    path('history/', ChatbotHistoryView.as_view(), name='chatbot-history'),
]

steps.instruction: Add path('api/chatbot/', include('chatbot.urls')), to the urlpatterns list. The resulting urlpatterns must be:
steps.resulting_code:
urlpatterns = [
    path('admin/', admin.site.urls),
    path('api/', include('core.urls')),
    path('api/courses/', include('courses.urls')),
    path('api/chatbot/', include('chatbot.urls')),
]

if settings.DEBUG:
    urlpatterns += static(settings.MEDIA_URL, document_root=settings.MEDIA_ROOT)

tasks.title: Backend: Implement chatbot/views.py with Anthropic Claude API
tasks.description: Replace the empty chatbot/views.py with a full implementation containing ChatbotMessageView (POST) and ChatbotHistoryView (GET).

tasks.title: GitHub Research: Find and evaluate an open-source React chat widget
tasks.description: Use WebSearch and WebFetch tools to find an open-source, well-designed React chat widget on GitHub. Analyze its code structure, then extract the core patterns (message list rendering, input box, typing indicator) to use as a foundation for LearningChatbot.js.
agent_instructions: Step 1 — Search GitHub using WebSearch with queries like: 'site:github.com react chat widget component floating MIT license lucide' or 'site:github.com open source react chatbot ui component floating bubble'

Step 2 — Identify 2-3 candidate repositories. Look for ones that are: MIT or Apache 2.0 licensed, use modern React hooks (useState, useEffect, useRef), have a floating bubble/widget pattern, are relatively small and self-contained (single component or a few files)
Step 3 — Use WebFetch to fetch the raw source code of the best candidate's main chat component file from GitHub (use raw.githubusercontent.com URL)
Step 4 — Study how the component handles: message state, scroll-to-bottom, typing indicators, input handling (Enter key), open/close toggle
Step 5 — Extract the best patterns from the GitHub component and use them as inspiration for implementing LearningChatbot.js in Task 5
Good search queries to try: 'react floating chat widget github', 'react chatbot ui component github floating bubble MIT', 'react-chatbot-kit github', 'chat-bubble react component github'
recommended_repos_to_investigate: Search: 'react chat widget floating bubble github stars:>100 language:JavaScript'
tasks.deliverable: A clear decision on which GitHub component's patterns to adopt, with extracted code snippets for: (1) message list rendering, (2) floating toggle button, (3) typing indicator animation

tasks.title: Frontend: Add chatbotAPI to frontend/src/services/api.js
tasks.description: Append the chatbotAPI export to the existing api.js. Do NOT modify any existing code — only add the new export block.

tasks.title: Frontend: Create LearningChatbot.js adapted from the GitHub research
tasks.description: Create the chat widget component. Base the code structure on the GitHub repository found in Task 4, but fully adapt it to: use our chatbotAPI from api.js, match the site's CSS custom property design system, use lucide-react icons, and handle auth state via useAuth().
requirements: Import icons from 'lucide-react': MessageCircle, X, Send, Bot, User, Loader2
State: isOpen (bool), messages (array), inputValue (string), isLoading (bool), historyLoaded (bool)
On mount when isOpen becomes true: call chatbotAPI.getHistory(courseId) to load prior messages
If no history, show welcome message: 'Hi! I am your AI learning assistant. Ask me anything about this course!'
On send: (1) append user message optimistically, (2) call chatbotAPI.sendMessage(text, courseId), (3) append bot reply, (4) handle errors gracefully
Typing indicator: animated bouncing dots while isLoading is true
Auto-scroll to bottom on new messages using useRef + scrollIntoView
If user is not authenticated: show disabled FAB with tooltip 'Login to use the AI assistant'
Floating position: fixed, bottom: 28px, right: 28px, zIndex: 1000
Chat window: fixed, bottom: 96px, right: 28px, width: 360px, maxHeight: 520px
User bubbles: indigo gradient (linear-gradient(135deg, var(--primary), #8b5cf6)), right-aligned
Bot bubbles: white with border, left-aligned
Header: indigo gradient with Bot avatar + 'AI Learning Assistant' + 'Powered by Claude'
Use ONLY inline styles or a <style> tag inside the component — no separate CSS file
Adapt the exact structural patterns (component architecture, scroll behavior, animation approach) from the GitHub repo found in Task 4
tasks.css_animation_for_typing: @keyframes typingBounce { 0%, 80%, 100% { transform: translateY(0); opacity: 0.4; } 40% { transform: translateY(-6px); opacity: 1; } } — apply to 3 dots with staggered animation-delay: 0s, 0.2s, 0.4s

tasks.title: Backend: Write tests for chatbot views with mocked Anthropic API
tasks.description: Replace chatbot/tests.py with comprehensive tests that mock the Anthropic client so no real API calls are made.
tasks.exact_code:
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock
from django.test import TestCase
from django.contrib.auth import get_user_model
from rest_framework.test import APIClient
from rest_framework import status
from .models import ChatSession, ChatMessage

User = get_user_model()


class ChatbotMessageViewTests(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.client = APIClient()
        self.user = User.objects.create_user(
            username='testuser', email='test@example.com', password='testpass123'
        )
        self.client.force_authenticate(user=self.user)
        self.url = '/api/chatbot/message/'

    def test_unauthenticated_returns_401(self):
        unauth = APIClient()
        res = unauth.post(self.url, {'message': 'Hello'}, format='json')
        self.assertEqual(res.status_code, status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED)

    def test_empty_message_returns_400(self):
        res = self.client.post(self.url, {'message': ''}, format='json')
        self.assertEqual(res.status_code, status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)

    def test_missing_message_field_returns_400(self):
        res = self.client.post(self.url, {}, format='json')
        self.assertEqual(res.status_code, status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST)

    @patch('chatbot.views.anthropic.Anthropic')
    def test_valid_message_returns_200_and_creates_db_records(self, mock_cls):
        mock_client = MagicMock()
        mock_cls.return_value = mock_client
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.content = [MagicMock(text='This is a test AI reply.')]
        mock_client.messages.create.return_value = mock_response

        res = self.client.post(self.url, {'message': 'What is a Django model?'}, format='json')

        self.assertEqual(res.status_code, status.HTTP_200_OK)
        self.assertEqual(res.data['reply'], 'This is a test AI reply.')
        self.assertIn('session_id', res.data)

        sessions = ChatSession.objects.filter(user=self.user)
        self.assertEqual(sessions.count(), 1)

        msgs = ChatMessage.objects.filter(session=sessions.first()).order_by('created_at')
        self.assertEqual(msgs.count(), 2)
        self.assertEqual(msgs[0].role, 'USER')
        self.assertEqual(msgs[0].content, 'What is a Django model?')
        self.assertEqual(msgs[1].role, 'BOT')
        self.assertEqual(msgs[1].content, 'This is a test AI reply.')

    @patch('chatbot.views.anthropic.Anthropic')
    def test_second_message_reuses_existing_session(self, mock_cls):
        mock_client = MagicMock()
        mock_cls.return_value = mock_client
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.content = [MagicMock(text='Reply.')]
        mock_client.messages.create.return_value = mock_response

        self.client.post(self.url, {'message': 'First'}, format='json')
        self.client.post(self.url, {'message': 'Second'}, format='json')

        self.assertEqual(ChatSession.objects.filter(user=self.user).count(), 1)
        self.assertEqual(ChatMessage.objects.count(), 4)  # 2 turns * 2 messages each


class ChatbotHistoryViewTests(TestCase):
    def setUp(self):
        self.client = APIClient()
        self.user = User.objects.create_user(
            username='histuser', email='hist@example.com', password='pass123'
        )
        self.client.force_authenticate(user=self.user)
        self.history_url = '/api/chatbot/history/'
        self.message_url = '/api/chatbot/message/'

    def test_empty_history_for_new_user(self):
        res = self.client.get(self.history_url)
        self.assertEqual(res.status_code, status.HTTP_200_OK)
        self.assertEqual(res.data['messages'], [])
        self.assertIsNone(res.data['session_id'])

    @patch('chatbot.views.anthropic.Anthropic')
    def test_history_returns_messages_after_chat(self, mock_cls):
        mock_client = MagicMock()
        mock_cls.return_value = mock_client
        mock_response = MagicMock()
        mock_response.content = [MagicMock(text='AI says hi.')]
        mock_client.messages.create.return_value = mock_response

        self.client.post(self.message_url, {'message': 'Hi'}, format='json')

        res = self.client.get(self.history_url)
        self.assertEqual(res.status_code, status.HTTP_200_OK)
        self.assertEqual(len(res.data['messages']), 2)
        self.assertEqual(res.data['messages'][0]['role'], 'USER')
        self.assertEqual(res.data['messages'][1]['role'], 'BOT')
        self.assertIsNotNone(res.data['session_id'])

tasks.title: Run migrations and verify the setup
tasks.description: Run Django migrations to ensure the ChatSession course FK is properly applied, then do a quick sanity check.

steps.expected_output: System check identified no issues (0 silenced).

critical_reminders_for_agent: Task 4 (GitHub Research) MUST be completed before Task 6 (LearningChatbot.js) — the component design should directly reflect what was found on GitHub
When fetching GitHub raw files, use raw.githubusercontent.com URLs, not the github.com HTML page
The chatbot model in views.py uses 'claude-opus-4-6' — do NOT change this model name
The existing api.js interceptor already attaches the JWT Bearer token — chatbotAPI calls will be authenticated automatically
Do NOT create a separate CSS file for the chatbot — use inline styles or a <style> tag inside the component
The frontend currently has NO chat/ directory under components/ — you must create it
After all code is written, inform the user they need to: (1) create .env with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, (2) run pip install anthropic python-dotenv, (3) run python manage.py migrate, then start both servers
post_implementation_checklist: 4. python manage.py test chatbot -v 2  (should show 7 tests passing)
6. npm start  (frontend on port 3000, in the frontend/ directory)
9. A purple chat bubble appears bottom-right — click it
10. Type 'Explain Django models' — a Claude response should appear
11. Refresh the page and reopen chat — history should persist
